pi_items.metadata_value: Class IA: FP<73 AND BP<100; Class IB: FP<73 AND BP>=100; Class IC: FP>=73 AND FP<100; Class II: FP>=100 AND FP<140; Class IIIA: FP>=140 AND FP<200; Class IIIB: FP>=200
pi_items.source: NFPA 30 Flammable and Combustible Liquids Code standard

pi_items.verified_value: https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/ - Real, public chemical hazard database maintained by NOAA
pi_items.source: Web verification of CAMEO Chemicals website

privileged_info_summary: This task provides ground truth data from CAMEO Chemicals (NOAA), a real public chemical hazard database. The data consists of Flash Point and Boiling Point values in Fahrenheit for six chemicals: Diethyl Ether (FP -49°F, BP 94.3°F), Toluene (FP 40°F, BP 231.1°F), o-Xylene (FP 90°F, BP 289-293°F), Acetic Acid (FP 102-104°F, BP 244°F), Benzaldehyde (FP 148°F, BP 354°F), and Glycerine (FP 320°F, BP 554°F). All claimed NFPA 30 Flammable Liquid Classifications (IA, IB, IC, II, IIIA, IIIB) are correct when applied to these values using the proper classification logic. The only minor discrepancy is Acetic Acid's flash point, claimed as 102°F but verified as 104°F in CAMEO Chemicals, a difference of 2°F within the 10-degree tolerance. The data is real, sourced from authoritative chemical safety databases (CAMEO Chemicals, NIOSH, ILO ICSC), not synthetic.
